signals.text: Adani Enterprises: completed acquisition of the remaining stake, via a company subsidiary, in IANS India for Rs 4.7 crore, making it a wholly owned subsidiary.

India’s composite PMI (manufacturing + services) fell to almost a 3 year low of 56.5 in March (vs 58.9 in Feb) as per preliminary estimates. Manufacturing PMI fell to 53.8 (vs 56.9 in Feb). Services PMI fell to 57.2 (vs 58.1 in Feb). This means economic activity grew significantly less in March than in Feb.
JSW Steel: completed acquisition of a 92.19% stake in Minas de Revuboe (a coal mining company in Mozambique) for $74.24 million.
OECD (Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development) has trimmed India’s FY27 growth forecast to 6.1%, down from 6.2% earlier, a revision due to rising global uncertainties.

Adani Ports: operationalized India’s first “Port of Refuge” to enhance maritime safety and emergency response infrastructure. It provides designated safe zones where distressed ships can seek shelter and receive emergency support like firefighting, salvage, and pollution control.

Coal India: plans to invest Rs 3,300 crore to set up 8 new coking coal washeries by FY30 to improve coal quality and reduce imports. It has also formed a 50:50 joint venture with Damodar Valley Corporation for power generation and related businesses
The Indian Rupee reached a record low, slipping past the Rs 93 per US$ mark for the first time.
The UK’s annual inflation rate stayed at 3% in Feb (same as Jan)
Nestle India: will invest around Rs 225 crore to add a new Munch production line at its Sanand plant with approximately 8,300 tonnes annual capacity.

India, the World Bank, and the Haryana government have signed a $300 million agreement to improve air quality in the state through policy reforms, investments, and stronger pollution monitoring systems.
The Indian Rupee fell to a record low, weakening beyond Rs 92.35 against the US Dollar, due to rising tensions due to the West Asia conflict and rising global crude oil prices.
India's natural gas comes from a much more concentrated list of sources: Qatar ~50%, USA ~8%, UAE ~8%, etc.
India's crude oil comes from various sources: Russia ~35%, Iraq ~20%, Saudi Arabia ~13%, UAE ~8%, etc.
The government has eased foreign direct investment (FDI) rules for countries sharing land borders with India, including China, so that certain investments no longer require government approval
India’s forex reserves fell by $11.68 billion to $716.81 billion in the week that ended on 6 March.
India’s passenger vehicle sales grew 10.6% year-on-year to 4.18 lakh units in Feb: SIAM

As of December 2025, the price per square foot for housing in Mumbai varies widely across neighbourhoods: Borivali West ₹25,443; Borivali East ₹25,570; Goregaon West ₹26,706; Goregaon East ₹37,687; Andheri West ₹41,733; Andheri East ₹26,059; Powai ₹35,643; Kurla ₹18,745; Bandra East ₹45,601; Dharavi ₹17,000; Wadala ₹39,046; Marine Lines ₹85,031; Colaba ₹46,444; Malabar Hill ₹1,17,725; Lower Parel ₹66,251; Worli ₹1,02,169; Dadar ₹39,007; Bandra West ₹88,422; Khar ₹51,697; and Juhu ₹83,238 per square foot.
The median U.S. home price now tops $405,000. The Urban Institute found that home sales prices have risen 80 percent since 2017, while wages have grown 38 percent.

The Ministry of Defence signed contracts worth Rs 5,083 crore for acquiring six Advanced Light Helicopters Mk-III for the Indian Coast Guard and Surface-to-Air Vertical Launch - Shtil missiles for the Indian Navy.
India and Canada on Monday agreed to a $1.9 billion, 10-year uranium supply deal for Indian nuclear power reactors.
India’s industrial activity slowed to a three-month low of 4.8% in January 2026.

Freight rates for very large crude carriers (VLCCs) transporting 2 million barrels of oil from the Middle East to China surged to a record high March 2, with the TD3 route climbing to W419 on the Worldscale index — equivalent to about $423,736 per day, according to LSEG data.
India’s real GDP grew 7.8% year-on-year in the Oct-Dec quarter (vs 8.4% in the previous quarter). GDP estimate for FY26 has been revised to 7.6%.
Trump, seeking executive power over elections, is urged to declare emergency
Education outlay in Haryana shows downward trend as debt and welfare expenditures rise.
Bharat Electronics: secured defence orders worth Rs 733 crore, including TR modules, communication equipment, radars, jammers and related systems.

Netflix announced Thursday that it will not raise its bid to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery, potentially paving the way for Paramount Skydance to swoop in and buy the company after a tense war between two media mega-corporations. Netflix struck an $83 billion deal in December to buy most of WBD, positioning the streaming giant to absorb HBO and its streaming service HBO Max, while also acquiring the famed Warner Bros. film and television studios. That deal would have seen cable networks including CNN spin off into an independent company.
New data from the Bureau of Economic Analysis shows that economic growth slowed to an annual rate of 1.4% in the final months of 2025.
The US has imposed preliminary 126% tariffs on solar imports from India, citing unfair subsidies for domestic manufacturing.
